macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: US Stock Indexes Rise on Tuesday Amid AI Optimism
items.summary: The S&P 500 climbed 0.8% to 6,890.07, Dow Jones rose 0.8% to 49,174.50, Nasdaq gained 1% to 22,863.68, and Russell 2000 increased 1.2% to 2,652.33, recovering from prior AI-related losses.

items.title: AMD Leads Rally with Multiyear AI Chip Deal for Meta Platforms
items.summary: AMD boosted markets after announcing a multiyear agreement to supply chips for Meta Platforms' AI initiatives, contributing to the broader tech recovery.

items.title: IBM Recovers from AI Selloff as Markets Rebound
items.summary: IBM regained ground after its worst loss since 2000 due to AI concerns, amid a market upswing driven by positive AI developments.

items.title: Weekly and YTD US Index Performance Mixed
items.summary: For the week, S&P 500 down 0.3%, Dow down 0.9%, Nasdaq down 0.1%, Russell 2000 down 0.4%; year-to-date, S&P up 0.7%, Dow up 2.3%, Nasdaq down 1.6%, Russell up 6.9%.

items.title: Daily Market Update for February 25, 2026
items.summary: M+ Morning Market Buzz provides latest trends on indices, commodities, and key stocks ahead of February 26 trading.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin Climbs 2.5% to $66,000 Amid Risk Appetite Return and Fed Anti-Debanking Proposal
items.summary: Bitcoin rose 2.5% over the past 24 hours to near $66,000, rebounding from $62,500 lows as equities rallied and the Federal Reserve proposed rules to combat crypto debanking. The Crypto Fear & Greed Index improved slightly to 11 but remains in extreme fear territory.[1]

items.title: Crypto.com Receives US Bank Charter Approval
items.summary: Crypto.com has won approval for a US bank charter, marking a significant regulatory milestone for the exchange amid ongoing White House meetings on crypto market structure legislation. This development highlights growing institutional integration in the sector.[5]

items.title: Ethereum Foundation Begins Staking Treasury Reserves with 2,016 ETH Deposit
items.summary: The Ethereum Foundation initiated staking of its treasury reserves, depositing 2,016 ETH with plans to scale to around 70,000 ETH and redirect rewards back to the treasury. This move underscores evolving network economics for DeFi infrastructure.[2]

items.title: US Spot Bitcoin ETFs See Continued Outflows Totaling $4.5 Billion in 2026
items.summary: US spot Bitcoin ETFs have experienced $4.5 billion in outflows this year, with BlackRock's IBIT losing over $2 billion in recent weeks, amid broader market corrections and softening institutional demand. February has seen extended drawdowns with Bitcoin down 29% year-to-date.[1][2][3]

items.title: Regulatory Tensions Rise Over Stablecoins in US Market Structure Negotiations
items.summary: Negotiations on US crypto market structure legislation remain tense, focusing on stablecoin rewards seen as threats by banks but vital for crypto platforms, alongside White House efforts to resolve issues with stakeholders. Global regulation increasingly impacts on-ramps and transparency.[2][5]

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: AI Chip Startups Secure $1.1B in VC Funding
items.summary: AI chip startups raised over $1.1 billion in venture capital on Tuesday, including MatX's funding for its LLM-optimized accelerator MatX One and SambaNova's $350M round backed by Intel, Vista Equity, and Cambium Capital to develop next-gen dataflow accelerators like the SN50.

items.title: BCG X and Nature Awards Launch AI for Discovery Award
items.summary: Boston Consulting Group’s AI Science Institute partnered with Nature Awards to create a global prize recognizing research teams using AI to advance solutions in health, sustainability, and manufacturing.

items.title: Harvard Study: AI Predicts 71% of Active Fund Managers' Trades
items.summary: A Harvard Business School-led study found a machine-learning model can predict about 71% of mutual fund managers' buy, sell, or hold decisions, questioning the value of active management fees amid shifts to index funds.

items.title: University of Minnesota Advances AI Tools for Agriculture
items.summary: Researchers at the University of Minnesota's AI-LEAF institute are using AI to analyze farm data for better productivity, sustainability, and pest control, including satellite-based detection of soybean aphid infestations.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Take-Two Interactive Earnings Mixed: GTA 6 Delay Concerns Resurface
items.summary: Take-Two shares dropped 9.5% despite beating earnings, with Bloomberg's Jason Schreier noting GTA 6 remains unfinished, raising fears of delays beyond Spring 2027. Capcom shares surged 10% on strong performance ahead of Resident Evil: Requiem launch on Feb 27.

items.title: Nintendo Switch 2 February 2026 Game Releases Announced
items.summary: New titles available now include DRAGON QUEST VII Reimagined, PGA TOUR 2K25, and TOKYO SCRAMBLE on Switch 2; upcoming releases feature Yakuza Kiwami 3 & Dark Ties (Feb 12), Fallout 4: Anniversary Edition (Feb 24), and Resident Evil Requiem (Feb 27).

items.title: BooztGames Expands Gaming Ad Network to 50+ Sites
items.summary: BooztGames announced growth surpassing 19M monthly ad impressions across over 50 gaming sites, targeting 18-34 gamers in US and Europe with 70+ campaigns for brands in gaming, esports, and hardware.

items.title: Xbox Leadership Reaffirms Commitment to First-Party Publishing
items.summary: Xbox CEO Asha Sharma and Chief Content Officer Matt Booty stated commitment to first-party games publishing amid multiplatform strategy discussions and analysis of Game Pass challenges post-price hikes.

items.title: Light & Wonder Reports Strong Q4 2025 Gaming Revenue Growth
items.summary: Light & Wonder's gaming revenue rose 6% with 25% operations growth, shipping over 12,300 units globally; settled Dragon Train litigation for $128M and hit iGaming records.

items.title: Galactic Space Guard Demo Released
items.summary: New demo for Galactic Space Guard features turn-based combat defending space stations from aliens, styled like 90s MTV visuals, emphasizing trade and profits.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.
